name: Nonlinear Manifold Learning Determines Microgel Size from Raman Spectroscopy
description: Raman spectra of 235 microgel samples with DLS-measured particle diameters (208–483 nm). Pretreatment: MinMax + Linear Fit, spectral range: FingerPrint. Task: predict particle diameter from Raman spectrum.
citeAs: @article{Koronaki_2024, title={Nonlinear Manifold Learning Determines Microgel Size from Raman Spectroscopy}, volume={20}, ISSN={1613-6829}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1002/smll.202311920}, DOI={10.1002/smll.202311920}, number={23}, journal={Small}, publisher={Wiley}, author={Koronaki, Eleni D. and Scholz, Jan G. T. and Modelska, Magdalena M. and Nayak, Prabhat K. and Viell, Jorn and Mitsos, Alexander and Barkley, Samuel}, year={2024}}
citation: Koronaki, E. D., Scholz, J. G. T., Modelska, M. M., Nayak, P. K., Viell, J., Mitsos, A., & Barkley, S. (2024). Nonlinear Manifold Learning Determines Microgel Size from Raman Spectroscopy. Small, 20(23), 2311920.

distribution.name: Nonlinear Manifold Learning Determines Microgel Size from Raman Spectroscopy
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Target value(s): concentration, physical property, or other continuous measurement.

rai:dataCollection: Offline Raman spectra of 47 N-isopropylacrylamide (NIPAM) microgel samples acquired at 20 °C in glass vials (identical fluid level) over two experimentation days without prior filtration or dialysis, using a Kaiser RXN2 Raman Analyzer with 40 s acquisition time; each sample measured three times. Microgels synthesised in a continuous flow reactor at varying temperature, flow rate, initiator, and surfactant concentrations.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Min-Max normalisation combined with linear fit baseline correction applied to fingerprint region spectra (850–1800 cm⁻¹, excluding O₂ region 1552–1560 cm⁻¹). Baseline subtracted first, then spectra rescaled to [0, 1] range. Pretreated data published at DOI 10.18154/RWTH-2023-05604.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Target values are hydrodynamic diameters (nm) measured by Dynamic Light Scattering (DLS) via Zetasizer Ultra (Malvern Panalytical) at 20 °C and scattering angle 90°, each repeated 4 times and analysed with ZS Xplorer software. DLS precision is approximately ±2%. The source publication used 40 training samples and 7 test samples.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information is included. All spectra originate from chemical samples without individual human identifiers.
rai:dataBiases: Offline measurements at 20 °C (microgels in swollen state) may not transfer to inline predictions at reaction temperatures (60–80 °C, collapsed state). Two distinct spectral clusters exist in the raw data attributed to different synthesis conditions. Multiple factors beyond particle size influence Raman spectra (monomer, inhibitor, surfactant concentrations). Small dataset (47 samples) limits statistical robustness and generalisability.
rai:dataLimitations: Small dataset (47 samples) is a restrictive factor; authors state findings cannot be generalised beyond the employed data sets. Samples from a single continuous flow reactor (RWTH Aachen University); generalisability to other reactor types or polymer systems not demonstrated. Offline measurements at 20 °C may not directly apply to inline process conditions at reaction temperature.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Enables data-driven polymer particle size monitoring from Raman spectroscopy, circumventing labor-intensive offline DLS measurements. Supports closed-loop control of polymerisation processes, contributing to consistent product quality in polymer manufacturing and potential application to crystallisation processes.
rai:dataUseCases: Microgel particle size prediction from Raman spectra; benchmarking nonlinear manifold learning (diffusion maps, Y-shaped autoencoders) against PLS and IHM+PLS baselines; dimensionality reduction for high-dimensional spectroscopic data; PAT for particle size monitoring in continuous polymer synthesis.
